expectations.text: simplify-step-present: Output shows /simplify step in the pipeline
expectations.evidence: Step 2 invokes /simplify via Skill(skill: "simplify", args: "internal/domain/consent/"). Applied fixes: removed 2 duplicated validation blocks, simplified error wrapping chain, removed unused import. Also re-invoked during remediation (Step 4.3) with minor cleanup.

expectations.text: remediation-loop-shown: Output shows a remediation loop when blocking findings occur
expectations.evidence: Step 4 REMEDIATION triggered by Code Reviewer (1 Critical), Security (1 High), and QA (Blocked at ~50% coverage). Full cycle: collect all blocking findings -> Developer fix -> /simplify on fix -> re-run Code Reviewer, Security, and QA -> all Approved. Structured as 'Remediation Cycle 1'.

expectations.text: remediation-max-2-cycles: Output mentions max 2 remediation cycles before escalation
expectations.evidence: Remediation is structured with numbered cycles. 'Remediation Cycle 1 Result: ALL BLOCKING FINDINGS RESOLVED' followed by 'ไม่ต้อง Cycle 2' (no need for Cycle 2), confirming the 2-cycle maximum framework.

Task Classification states 'Workflow Selected: Code Review' with reason: 'User ขอ review code ก่อน merge โดยระบุ 3 concerns ชัดเจน: convention compliance, security, test coverage ซึ่งตรงกับ Code Review workflow'.

expectations.text: parallel-review-step: Developer + Security + QA run in parallel
expectations.evidence: Step 3 header states 'Developer + Security + QA -- PARALLEL' with explicit note: 'ทั้ง 3 agents run พร้อมกัน ไม่มี dependency ระหว่างกัน'. Steps 3a (Developer), 3b (Security), 3c (QA) all delegated in parallel.

expectations.text: code-reviewer-first: Code Reviewer runs before parallel agents
expectations.evidence: Step 1 is 'Code Reviewer -- Check Convention Compliance' which runs solo. Step 2 is /simplify. Step 3 is the parallel step. Code Reviewer context is passed to all Step 3 agents as 'Context from Prior Agents: Code Reviewer Findings'.
